summary: Iteration 4 — Validated both fixes from the request: (1) JSX early-return null in /app/frontend/app/dashboard/admin/settings/page.tsx prevents any flash of admin UI / 'Authorising…' text before the role gate resolves; (2) test_generate_problem_forbidden_for_non_admin now signs up a fresh unique-email regular user and asserts 401/403 (PASSES). Full backend pytest suite is now 36/36 PASS (was 35/36 in iteration 3). Frontend role gate verified end-to-end: (a) superadmin qa@codespectra.dev → /dashboard/admin/settings?section=integrations renders the Razorpay configuration card with all three inputs (rzp-section, rzp-key-id-input, rzp-key-secret-input, rzp-webhook-input) and NO 'Authorising…' flash; (b) freshly signed-up regular user (role='user') hitting the same URL → at 150ms after mount the page body contained no rzp-* elements and no 'Authorising' text (just the admin-settings layout shell from the layout file), and was redirected to /dashboard within a second. Backend direct check: authenticated superadmin POST /api/ai/generate-problem does NOT return 401/403 (the LLM starts generating — connection times out as expected); authenticated regular user gets 401/403. Made one test-script fix: added the `Origin: http://localhost:3000` header to the requests.Session() inside test_generate_problem_forbidden_for_non_admin because Better Auth rejects POST /api/auth/* without an Origin header (MISSING_OR_NULL_ORIGIN 403) — this was a test-only correction, no app code touched.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/app/dashboard/admin/settings/page.tsx — the `if (!gate.ready) return null` guard (line 327) correctly suppresses the half-mounted form during gate resolution. Verified by sampling DOM at 150ms after mount for a non-admin user: no rzp-* selectors and no 'Authorising' text are present. The secondary 'Loading settings…' spinner (line 329-336) still renders normally once gate.ready=true and `loading=true` while /api/admin/server-secrets is in flight — this is the intended UX.

/app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — test_generate_problem_forbidden_for_non_admin now self-provisions a unique regular user per run (timestamped email), which keeps it idempotent and not coupled to the seeded superadmin. Recommend documenting in the test docstring that any client hitting Better Auth POST endpoints (sign-up/sign-in) MUST include the Origin header — easy footgun for future test authors.

Better Auth 403 MISSING_OR_NULL_ORIGIN behaviour is good security posture but inconsistent with the pytest fixture `auth_session` which works without Origin only because of `cookies.txt`/session reuse on subsequent requests. The fixture currently sets the Origin header indirectly via the cookied requests.Session; document this expectation explicitly.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — added `Origin: http://localhost:3000` header to the requests.Session() inside test_generate_problem_forbidden_for_non_admin (line 412) so Better Auth doesn't reject the sign-in with 403 MISSING_OR_NULL_ORIGIN. No app code modified.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — superadmin sees Razorpay form with all 3 inputs and no Authorising flash; regular user gets no admin UI flash and is redirected to /dashboard

test_credentials: qa@codespectra.dev / QApass123! (superadmin) + qa-regular-<timestamp>@example.com / RegPass123! (created on the fly by the role-gate test)
seed_data_creation: Each run of test_generate_problem_forbidden_for_non_admin creates a unique regular user via POST /api/auth/sign-up/email. Frontend test 2 also created one regular user for the redirect verification.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All carry-over items from iteration_3 are now resolved. Backend suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is fully green (36/36 in ~142s). Test against http://localhost:3000 + http://localhost:8001 directly. Better Auth POST /api/auth/* endpoints REQUIRE an `Origin: http://localhost:3000` header (otherwise 403 MISSING_OR_NULL_ORIGIN). The frontend role-gate page guard pattern `if (!gate.ready) return null` is now the standard for admin pages — verified working. Sign-in path is /auth/login (NOT /auth/sign-in — that 404s).
rca of the issue: Two fixes landed in this iteration: (A) /app/frontend/app/dashboard/admin/settings/page.tsx — added `if (!gate.ready) return null` at line 327 to suppress the half-mounted form during the brief window where useRoleGate is still fetching /api/auth/get-session. Without this, a regular user hitting the URL would see a flash of admin UI (and trigger /api/admin/* fetches that would fail with 401/403) before being redirected. (B) /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py::test_generate_problem_forbidden_for_non_admin — the seeded QA user is now superadmin, so reusing the `auth_session` fixture (as before) incorrectly hit the LLM success path and timed out. Fix: sign up a fresh unique-email regular user inside the test and assert 401/403 on POST /api/ai/generate-problem. The testing-agent-applied tweak in this iteration was adding the `Origin` header to the request session (Better Auth requirement).
